name: 17-invalid-cdx-sidecar-hash
description: CycloneDX document's externalReferences[].hashes[].content does not equal sha256 of the canonical AIBOM sidecar bytes. Verifier rejects at hash-match stage per schema-rule.
notes: The AIBOM sidecar is a valid JCS-canonical document and passes schema-validation + canonicalization stages. The CycloneDX document itself is also valid CycloneDX 1.5. The failure is at hash-match: the externalReferences hash (all zeros) does not match the computed sha256 of the sidecar's canonical bytes (8eb298a912f29cbac119e937b75d3a836d80233655f1e097c231ee4cd24bba95). This fixture also covers the Q3 array-order violation class: if an emitter distributed a semantically-equivalent-but-non-canonical sidecar, the CDX hash (computed over canonical bytes) would fail the same way — exactly per schema-rule's rule that the referenced artifact IS the canonical bytes.
